title: Motivators at work
paperName: Paper 3 - Specialist Options: Approaches, Issues and Debates
summary: 9990 Organisational — job enrichment, empowerment, and practical motivation strategies.
learningObjectives: Explain job enrichment and job enlargement.
Analyse Hackman & Oldham's job characteristics model.
Apply motivation theory to workplace case studies.

simpleExplanation.title: Making work motivating
simpleExplanation.summary: Organisations boost motivation through enriched jobs, autonomy, feedback, and fair pay — matching strategies to what theory predicts works.
steps: JCM — skill variety, task identity, significance.
Autonomy and feedback — core psychological states.
Pay, praise, promotion — extrinsic vs intrinsic.

flashcards.back: Enlargement adds more tasks at the same level; enrichment adds responsibility, planning, and feedback — deeper not just wider.

flashcards.front: Hackman & Oldham's five core job characteristics?
flashcards.back: Skill variety, task identity, task significance, autonomy, feedback from the job itself.
Experienced meaningfulness, experienced responsibility, knowledge of results — mediate characteristics → outcomes.
Individual difference — high GNS workers respond more strongly to enriched jobs.
Pay satisfies hygiene needs (Herzberg) but may not sustain intrinsic motivation; can even crowd out internal drive.
Giving employees authority, resources, and confidence to make decisions — boosts autonomy and accountability.

sections.content: **Motivators at work** (4.1.3) completes 4.1. Link **Hackman & Oldham JCM** to practical HR policies — enrichment beats simple task rotation.

sections.question: A factory replaces repetitive assembly with 'cells' where each team builds a whole product, tracks quality, and meets weekly with engineers. Absenteeism falls and output rises. Explain using JCM and Herzberg.
sections.solution:
**Hackman & Oldham:** Cell design increases **skill variety** (multiple operations), **task identity** (complete product), and **task significance** (visible quality impact). Teams gain **autonomy** over pacing and problem-solving plus **feedback** from quality data — producing **meaningfulness**, **responsibility**, and **knowledge of results**.

**Herzberg:** The change adds **motivators** (responsibility, achievement, recognition from quality gains) not just **hygiene** improvements.

**McClelland:** Appeals to **nAch** through measurable team targets.

**Evaluation:** **Growth need strength** matters — some workers preferred predictable routine. **Job enrichment** costs training and capital; gains may reflect **Hawthorne** attention as well as JCM. Combine with **fair pay** (hygiene) so basics are secure before expecting intrinsic motivation.
